commands.review_summary:
Adds canonical memory role slots and per-agent memory slot overrides, wiring them through config validation, schemas, docs, plugin activation/startup/runtime resolution, doctor repair/status, skills/hooks, uninstall/update cleanup, and gateway plugin subagent completion.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/API PR rather than a current-main bug reproduction, and current main lacks the requested role-slot capability.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human maintainer/API review and exact-head contributor proof; automation cannot decide the public taxonomy or reproduce the contributor's private layered memory setup.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Exact-head remediation proof for PR #88504 at `c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b`. This proof covers canonical per-agent `agents.list[].plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` owner preservation, legacy memory compatibility, disabled `none` recall slots, missing/stale plugin validation behavior, doctor migration/repair behavior, changed-path WebSocket payload-cap remediation, plugin SDK subagent gateway-dispatch completion bookkeeping, and an explicit rollback/upgrade matrix from the last public release.
- Real environment tested: Dev-01 canonical checkout plus Orion/Nano-01 Linux rollback/upgrade test box. Nano was rolled back to public release `v2026.5.28` (peeled commit `e93216080aa1f425d3ab127014603eba8e365b2d`) and then upgraded directly to PR head `c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b`. Runtime logs were redacted for private host, path, token, and network values.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: on Dev-01, ran full gateway-methods Vitest, focused slot/plugin-helper Vitest, focused type-aware oxlint for touched files, `git diff --check`, and one live `openclaw agent --json` turn. On Orion/Nano-01, checked out `v2026.5.28`, installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, captured release `config validate`, `doctor --fix`, `status`, `plugins list`, and `models status`; then checked out PR head `c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b`, reinstalled, ran `config validate`, `doctor --fix`, `status`, `plugins list`, and `models status`; restored the intended pre-test slot matrix after proving old-release doctor strips unsupported future slot keys; reran PR `config validate`, `doctor --fix`, `status`, `plugins list`, and `models status`; then ran five real `openclaw agent --json` GitHub Copilot turns for `main`, `honcho-agent`, `dreamer-agent`, `split-agent`, and `disabled-recall-agent`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted proof bundle id `pr88504-upgrade-matrix-20260601T230545Z`; current PR head `c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b`; five restored live-agent markers `RESTORED_LIVE_UPGRADE_PROOF_OK`; Honcho health `ok`; GitHub Copilot auth status `ok`; LosslessClaw enabled as `contextEngine`; focused and gateway-methods regression gates passed as copied output below.

```text
head=c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b
release_base=v2026.5.28 e93216080aa1f425d3ab127014603eba8e365b2d
proof_bundle=pr88504-upgrade-matrix-20260601T230545Z
upgrade_path=release checkout -> release doctor/status/models -> PR checkout -> PR doctor/status/models -> restored slot matrix -> five live agent turns
```

```text
Dev-01 source gates:
- gateway-methods Vitest: passed, 55 files, 1043 tests
- focused slots/plugin helpers Vitest: passed, 2 files, 31 tests
- focused type-aware oxlint for touched files: passed
- git diff --check: passed
- openclaw agent --json --agent openclaw-dev: passed, embedded runner, marker LIVE_PROOF_OK
```

```text
Nano upgrade matrix:
- checkout v2026.5.28: passed
- release pnpm install --frozen-lockfile: passed
- release version: OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (e932160)
- release config validate against PR-era role-slot config: failed as expected on agents.list role-slot objects and plugins.slots role keys
- release doctor --fix: passed, and proved old-release doctor strips unsupported future memory role slots from this PR-era config
- release status/plugins/models: passed
- checkout PR head c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b: passed
- PR pnpm install --frozen-lockfile: passed
- PR config validate: passed
- PR doctor --fix: passed
- PR status/plugins/models: passed
- restored intended pre-test slot matrix after destructive old-release doctor compatibility check: passed
- restored PR config validate/doctor/status/plugins/models: passed
```

```text
restored_slot_matrix:
- global plugins.slots.memory.recall = memory-core
- global plugins.slots.memory.dreaming = memory-core
- global plugins.slots.memory.userModel = openclaw-honcho
- global plugins.slots.contextEngine = lossless-claw
- honcho-agent memory.recall/userModel = openclaw-honcho
- dreamer-agent memory.recall/dreaming = memory-core; userModel = openclaw-honcho
- split-agent memory.recall = memory-core; memory.capture = active-memory; userModel = openclaw-honcho
- disabled-recall-agent memory.recall = none; memory.capture = active-memory; userModel = openclaw-honcho
```

```text
ancillary readiness:
- github-copilot auth: models status ok; one static github-copilot profile available; missingProvidersInUse=[]; unusableProfiles=[]
- local Honcho: http health returned {"status":"ok"}; openclaw-honcho enabled
- lossless-claw: enabled and selected as contextEngine
- five test agents: main, honcho-agent, dreamer-agent, split-agent, disabled-recall-agent present after restore and doctor
```

```text
restored_live_github_copilot_turns:
- main: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, runner=embedded, tools=memory_search, marker=RESTORED_LIVE_UPGRADE_PROOF_OK
- honcho-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, runner=embedded, tools=session_status, marker=RESTORED_LIVE_UPGRADE_PROOF_OK
- dreamer-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, runner=embedded, tools=memory_search, marker=RESTORED_LIVE_UPGRADE_PROOF_OK
- split-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, runner=embedded, tools=memory_search, marker=RESTORED_LIVE_UPGRADE_PROOF_OK
- disabled-recall-agent: exit=0, provider=github-copilot, model=gpt-5.4-mini, runner=embedded, tools=memory_search, marker=RESTORED_LIVE_UPGRADE_PROOF_OK
```

```text
selector_cases_covered:
- canonical agents.list[].plugins.slots["memory.recall"] protects that agent-scoped recall owner when selecting a new global memory.recall owner
- legacy agents.list[].plugins.slots.memory protects that accepted compatibility recall owner when selecting a new global memory.recall owner
- canonical global plugins.slots["memory.recall"] remains valid after PR upgrade and doctor
- disabled per-agent memory.recall = none remains valid after PR upgrade and doctor
- missing/stale plugin validation and doctor cleanup paths remain covered by focused regression/CI gates
- old-release doctor behavior against future PR-era slot keys is explicitly captured and treated as an upgrade-risk artifact, not a valid downgrade path
- noServer WebSocket fixtures in plugin capability auth tests now set maxPayload=MAX_PREAUTH_PAYLOAD_BYTES
- plugin SDK subagent agent runs registered by the gateway complete from the gateway dispatch result and no longer try unauthenticated agent.wait RPC cleanup
```

- Observed result after fix: the current PR head validates and doctors the intended memory role-slot matrix after direct upgrade from the last public release. Global recall, per-agent recall overrides, disabled recall, capture/dreaming/user-model role slots, Honcho, LosslessClaw, GitHub Copilot auth, and all five real agent turns survived the restored PR-side doctor/status/model checks.
- What was not tested: local Nano Vitest transform for the deterministic matrix did not finish in a useful window and was terminated after staying CPU-bound without output; equivalent focused source gates had already passed on Dev-01 and CI. The old-release doctor pass was intentionally captured as destructive for future role-slot keys, then the pre-test config was restored before final PR-side doctor and live proof. Gateway service installation/start was not performed because the test box reports gateway service not installed; proof uses source CLI and embedded agent turns.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local opengrep was not installed on Nano, so changed-path scan verification remains GitHub Actions evidence. Runtime proof logs were redacted to avoid private host, path, token, and network values.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current PR branch head:

- `c971fe1376d17c6c97161d7b36173a7b142ac31b` (`fix(gateway): complete plugin subagent dispatch runs`)
- Branch: `kklouzal:feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`
- Base target: `openclaw/openclaw:main`

Latest exact-head proof gates:

- Full gateway-methods Vitest: passed, 55 files, 1043 tests.
- Focused slot/plugin-helper Vitest: passed, 2 files, 31 tests.
- Focused changed-file oxlint including plugin-SDK boundary dts: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- Orion/Nano-01 rollback/upgrade matrix from `v2026.5.28` to PR head: passed after restoring the intended pre-test slot config following the intentionally captured old-release doctor compatibility check.
- Orion/Nano-01 restored PR-side `config validate`, `doctor --fix`, `status`, `plugins list`, and `models status`: passed.
- Five real GitHub Copilot embedded agent turns across `main`, `honcho-agent`, `dreamer-agent`, `split-agent`, and `disabled-recall-agent`: passed.
target.author: kklouzal
